_about: Ground-truth reproduction targets for the EconGym v0 Bertrand environment (package `econgym`). Numbers were produced by RUNNING the ORIGINAL Paper-1 code in the algorithmic-bertrand-rl repository (config.py + simulation.py) via _groundtruth.py. The econgym re-implementation must reproduce these AGGREGATE statistics within the stated tolerances. This is the BINDING acceptance for the reproduce test.

paper.title: The Two Faces of Algorithmic Collusion: Memory, Spuriousness, and a Model-Free Entropy Diagnostic in Homogeneous-Good Bertrand Competition
paper.author: Shubh Lamba

config._note: Fast (~90s) representative reproduction of the paper's HEADLINE baseline. Uses BASE params from config.py EXCEPT K, which is set to the paper's stated baseline grid K=21 (config.py's BASE dict lists K=7 as the COARSE-grid contrast; paper Section 4: 'I use K=21 as the baseline'). All other params are BASE verbatim.
config.seeds: range(0, 50)  -> seeds 0..49 for EACH learner (independent RNG streams; same seed integers, entirely separate games)
config.H_star_source: Paper's published data-derived antimode (Section 4, H*=0.288). FIXED in the reproduce test rather than re-derived per-run: the K=21 collusive-share separation is robust for H* in [0.20, 0.288] (mean 0.92-0.96 vs Q 0.04) and only erodes above ~0.40 as Q's chaotic cluster (H~0.51) leaks in.

market_benchmarks._note: Deterministic given the grid; must match exactly.
market_benchmarks.nash_profit_note: K=21 grid includes p=1.0=c, so p_comp=c and pi^N=(1-1)/2=0.

collusion_intensity_delta_index._desc: Mean collusion index Delta over all 50 seeds (unconditional). Delta=(pi_bar - piN)/(piM - piN) over the final T0=2000 periods. On INTENSITY, Q-learning colludes HARDER than mean-based (paper: 'less often but harder').

delta_two_sample_test._desc: Independent two-sample z of (mean_based_delta - q_delta) across the 50-seed arrays. Strongly NEGATIVE: Q's per-run Delta exceeds mean-based's.

collusion_frequency_regime_share._desc: Fraction of runs classified regime=='Collusive' (H_norm < H* AND p_bar >= p_star_factor*c), at H*=0.288. This is the PAPER'S HEADLINE: the stateless mean-based learner colludes MORE OFTEN than the stateful Q-learner. On FREQUENCY, mean-based >> Q.

share_two_proportion_test._desc: Two-proportion z of (mean_collusive_share - q_collusive_share) at H*=0.288 (48/50 vs 2/50). Strongly POSITIVE: mean-based colludes MORE OFTEN, strongly significant. THIS is the 'mean-based colludes more' signature.

convergence_fraction._desc: Fraction of runs with non-circular convergence (greedy policy stable over final 10% for both firms, tol=0.99). Both learners converge at similar rates -> the mean-based advantage in FREQUENCY is NOT a non-convergence artifact.

normalised_entropy_per_learner._desc: Mean K-normalised Shannon entropy (Miller-Madow corrected), averaged over both firms, over the final T0 window, averaged across the 50 seeds. Mean-based collapses to a stable supra-competitive price (LOW entropy); Q-learning stays high-entropy / chaotic at T=30000.

acceptance_summary._desc: The reproduce test (tests/test_reproduce_paper1.py) PASSES iff ALL of the following hold, with econgym re-running the exact config above.
headline_invariants: SIGN: mean-based colludes MORE OFTEN (frequency): mean_collusive_share >> q_collusive_share, share z >> 0.
SIGN: Q-learning colludes HARDER (intensity): q_delta_mean > mean_delta_mean, delta z << 0.
Both learners converge (~0.88): the frequency gap is not a convergence artifact.
Entropy separates the two: mean-based low (~0.05, stable price), Q-learning high (~0.51, chaotic).

faithfulness_notes.rng: numpy np.random.default_rng(seed). Q-learning reproduces the original BYTE-FOR-BYTE (identical RNG call order). Mean-based is reproduced at the AGGREGATE level only: the clean BertrandEnv.reset() draws the initial price profile via rng.integers(0,K,size=n) for ALL agent types (faithful to the Q path), whereas the ORIGINAL run_meanbased consumed NO init draw. Measured effect of this single-draw offset on mean-based aggregates: delta 0.0042, entropy 0.0055, conv 0.02 -- comfortably inside tolerances (see _offset_check.py).
faithfulness_notes.rng_call_order_qlearning: 1) rng.normal(0,1e-6,(K,K)) for agent 0; 2) same for agent 1; 3) rng.integers(0,K,size=n) for initial prices. Then per step, IN AGENT ORDER 0,1: rng.random() (always, for the epsilon test), then rng.integers(K) ONLY if exploring; exploit = np.argmax (deterministic first-max, NO rng tie-break).
faithfulness_notes.rng_call_order_meanbased: No init draws in the original (env.reset draw is the one intended offset). Per step, IN AGENT ORDER 0,1: rng.random() (always), then rng.integers(K) if exploring, ELSE _randargmax(value_row, rng) which draws rng.integers(n_ties) ONLY when the greedy max is tied (all-zero value table at t=0 => full tie => a draw). This randomized tie-break MUST be preserved.
faithfulness_notes.two_faces_warning: The prompt's phrasing 'mean-based colludes MORE than Q-learning' is TRUE on the FREQUENCY/regime-share metric (its home; z>>0) but the OPPOSITE holds on the raw delta_index INTENSITY metric (Q higher; z<<0). This is not a contradiction -- it is the paper's central 'two faces' result ('the stateless learner colludes more often but weakly; the stateful learner less often but harder'). Both are recorded and both are binding. Do NOT 'fix' the delta z to be positive.
faithfulness_notes.runtime: ~90s for the full reproduce config (100 runs) with the original tight numpy loops. Budget the econgym reproduce test at <=3 min to absorb Agent-abstraction per-step overhead.

cross_check_K7._desc: NON-BINDING. K=7 (config.py's true BASE grid, the coarse-grid contrast), seeds 0..49, T=30000. Documents the grid-resolution effect the paper emphasizes: at the coarse grid Q-learning colludes far more often (its competitive best response is easier to over/under-shoot). Recorded for faithfulness documentation only; the reproduce test uses K=21. Collusive shares here use the K=7 pooled data-derived antimode H*=0.2375 (not 0.288).
cross_check_K7.note: At K=7 the mean-more-often gap is smaller (mean 0.90 vs Q 0.50) and Q's converged collusion is intense (Delta 0.39) -- a messier signature than K=21, which is why K=21 is the reproduce grid.
